skill.name: architecture-diagram
skill.description: Create polished architecture diagrams as standalone HTML files with inline SVG, embedded CSS, and a dark technical visual style.
skill.summary: Generate dark-themed technical architecture diagrams as self-contained HTML files.
skill.author: Cocoon-AI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported network access, command execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns. Review found the command execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance alerts are false positives from markdown backticks, CSS/SVG examples, and placeholder text; the only confirmed issue is a Google Fonts dependency in the template.

low_findings.title: External Font Dependency in Generated HTML
low_findings.description: The template and instructions load JetBrains Mono from Google Fonts. This is a real external network dependency that can disclose browser requests to a third-party font service, but it does not execute scripts or transmit skill data by itself.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL is directly present in the HTML template and documentation. The impact is limited because it is a font stylesheet request, not arbitrary code execution or data exfiltration.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Misread as Command Execution
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution locations are markdown inline code and fenced HTML or SVG examples. No evidence found of executable Ruby, shell scripts, subprocess calls, or user-controlled command execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is markdown documentation and the flagged characters delimit examples or inline literals. There is no runtime wrapper that would execute those snippets.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts do not correspond to a cryptographic implementation. The referenced lines are description text, section headings, or HTML placeholder text, with no hash, cipher, or password handling code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of each referenced line found natural-language or template content only. No evidence found of MD5, SHA-1, RC4, DES, or similar weak algorithm use.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Pattern
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance alerts point to layout guidance, CSS classes, and SVG background or container examples. No evidence found of hostname, user, process, network, or environment enumeration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The locations are visual-design instructions or static SVG and CSS template lines. They contain no commands or APIs that inspect the host system.

content.value_statement: Technical teams need clear diagrams that show systems, boundaries, and data flow. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code produce self-contained HTML diagrams with inline SVG and consistent styling.
actual_capabilities: Generates standalone HTML files with embedded CSS and inline SVG diagrams.
Provides a dark visual system for frontend, backend, database, cloud, security, and messaging components.
Defines reusable SVG patterns for component boxes, arrows, security groups, regions, legends, and info cards.
Guides spacing, z-order, and legend placement to reduce diagram overlap.
Supports architecture, infrastructure, cloud, security, and network topology diagrams.
limitations: The template loads Google Fonts unless the user removes or localizes that dependency.
The skill does not discover infrastructure automatically from cloud accounts or repositories.
Complex diagrams still require user-provided system details and review for accuracy.
The output is static HTML and SVG, not an interactive modeling tool.

use_cases.title: Document a Product Architecture
use_cases.description: Create a concise diagram for services, storage, clients, and integration points in a product design document.

use_cases.title: Explain Cloud Infrastructure
use_cases.description: Show cloud regions, service boundaries, load balancers, databases, and network paths for deployment planning.

use_cases.title: Review Security Boundaries
use_cases.description: Map authentication flows, security groups, protected resources, and trust boundaries before an implementation review.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Service Diagram
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a small application diagram.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a standalone HTML architecture diagram for a web app with users, a React frontend, an API service, and PostgreSQL. Use the architecture-diagram skill style.

prompt_templates.title: Cloud Deployment Diagram
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a dark-themed HTML and SVG diagram for an AWS deployment with CloudFront, S3, an application load balancer, two backend services, RDS, and an external auth provider.

prompt_templates.title: Security Flow Diagram
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request focused on boundaries and authentication.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an architecture diagram that highlights OAuth login, JWT validation, private service traffic, database access, and security group boundaries. Include a legend and three summary cards.

prompt_templates.title: Production System Diagram
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for a detailed review artifact.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a self-contained HTML architecture diagram for a multi-region SaaS platform. Include clients, CDN, API tier, event bus, workers, storage, monitoring, trust boundaries, and labeled data flows.

output_examples.input: A startup needs a diagram for a React app, API server, PostgreSQL database, and Auth0 login.
output_examples.output: A standalone dark HTML diagram with labeled frontend, backend, database, and auth components, plus arrows showing HTTPS and token validation flows.

output_examples.input: A platform team wants to explain an AWS deployment with CloudFront, S3, load balancing, services, and RDS.
output_examples.output: A static HTML and SVG architecture view with an AWS region boundary, cloud service boxes, backend services, database, legend, and summary cards.

output_examples.input: A security team needs a review diagram for protected APIs, private databases, and authentication flows.
output_examples.output: A security-focused diagram that uses dashed boundaries, rose-colored auth paths, labeled ports, and concise notes for review meetings.

best_practices: Provide the component list, deployment environment, and main data flows before asking for the diagram.
Ask for trust boundaries, protocols, and ports when the diagram will support security review.
Review the generated labels and positions before sharing the HTML with stakeholders.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill as a source of truth for infrastructure inventory.
Do not include secrets, private keys, tokens, or customer data in diagram labels.
Do not publish the default Google Fonts dependency in restricted environments without approval.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates standalone HTML architecture diagrams with embedded CSS and inline SVG graphics.

faq.question: Does it require JavaScript?
faq.answer: No. The documented output uses static HTML, CSS animations, and inline SVG.

faq.question: Can it generate cloud architecture diagrams?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes visual patterns for cloud services, regions, databases, security groups, and service flows.

faq.question: Can it inspect my cloud account automatically?
faq.answer: No. Users must provide the system details, services, boundaries, and relationships to include.

faq.question: Is the output self-contained?
faq.answer: Mostly. The template embeds CSS and SVG, but it references Google Fonts unless that link is removed.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
